passage.title: Team Role Board Redesign
passage.text: The class teamwork team wanted younger students to understand group roles more easily. At first, the team made one role chart with many decorated boxes and small names, but students forgot which job belonged to them. Several first graders said "I can read the board, but I still forget my job." After hearing that, the team gave each role one strong symbol and one short title and placed matching role cards on each table. They also set a quick-check sign in the middle of the room so students could look down and see the same role card at their own table. On the next test day, more teams started their projects without teacher help during the next lesson. The team noticed that clear role clues helped more than decorative boxes. Because of that result, the class planned to use the same role system for other group projects.

questions.prompt: What is the passage mainly about?
choices: Improving teamwork signs so younger students can share roles
Painting a room with brighter colors
Buying more books for a library sale
Writing long reports about class rules
questions.rationale: The whole passage focuses on improving a system for younger students.

questions.prompt: What problem did students mention about the first plan?
choices: The labels were too small to read quickly
The first plan had too many steps
Students could not tell what to do next
Students forgot which job belonged to them
questions.rationale: The early part of the passage explains what did not work well.

questions.prompt: What did the team place on each table?
choices: Short signs
Color blocks
Matching role cards
Larger arrows
questions.rationale: The middle of the passage tells what the team added or changed.

questions.prompt: What does mix-ups mean in the sixth sentence?
choices: A person who leads the school
Times when things get confused or switched
A room that is easy to clean
A rule about lunch time
questions.rationale: The word meaning comes from the way the passage uses it.

questions.prompt: Why did the new design work better for younger students?
choices: The new board made each role and step easier to see
It made the room much larger
It reduced the number of students
It removed every sign from the area
questions.rationale: You have to connect the redesign changes with the later results.

questions.prompt: What will the team most likely do next?
choices: Use the same clearer style in more places
Remove every sign from the room
Ask students to memorize everything
Use the same clear role system for more projects
questions.rationale: The last sentence points to the team's next likely action.
